title: Persistent storage risk card for n8n on PaaS
acceptanceConclusion: 服務能啟動不代表 state 能在 redeploy 後存活；必須驗證 PostgreSQL、user folder volume、N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY、binary data path、backup/restore、stable WEBHOOK_URL。

riskCards.risk: n8n writes state into an ephemeral filesystem.
symptoms: Redeploy returns to first-run setup screen.
Workflows or credentials disappear after restart.
SQLite database lives inside the container layer.
riskCards.impact: Loss of workflows, credentials, executions, and operator trust.
firstChecks: Does the platform document the filesystem as ephemeral by default?
Is DB_TYPE set to postgresdb?
Is the n8n user folder mounted to durable storage?
mitigation: Use PostgreSQL for n8n main state.
Mount persistent storage for /home/node/.n8n or an explicitly configured N8N_USER_FOLDER.
Run a redeploy persistence smoke test before production.

riskCards.risk: PostgreSQL exists, but n8n user folder and encryption material are ignored.
symptoms: Workflows remain but credentials cannot decrypt.
Binary file workflows fail after redeploy.
Source-control or local-file features lose expected files.
riskCards.impact: Partial state survival creates harder incidents than a clean failure.
firstChecks: Is N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY fixed in platform secrets?
Does the deployment rely on n8n auto-generated settings in ephemeral storage?
Do workflows produce binary files on local disk?
mitigation: Set N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY explicitly and back it up.
Persist n8n user folder or configure external storage where available.
Document which state lives in DB and which state lives in files.

riskCards.risk: A volume exists, but PostgreSQL backup and restore are not designed.
symptoms: Redeploy works but a DB corruption or deletion has no recovery path.
Snapshots exist but nobody has tested restore.
Execution history grows until DB storage pressure appears.
riskCards.impact: A single DB incident can erase the automation system.
firstChecks: Is PostgreSQL managed or template-based/unmanaged?
Where are backups stored?
When was the last restore test?
mitigation: Enable managed database backups or external backups.
Write a restore runbook.
Schedule a restore drill for a staging database.

riskCards.risk: Production webhooks run on a free or sleeping service tier.
symptoms: Webhook providers timeout on cold starts.
Scheduled workflows miss expected timing.
Free database expires or becomes unavailable.
riskCards.impact: Automation becomes unreliable even when configuration is correct.
firstChecks: Does the web service sleep after inactivity?
Does the database expire after a fixed period?
Are production workflows time-sensitive?
mitigation: Use paid always-on compute for production.
Use paid persistent database resources.
Create alerts for service suspension, sleep, and database expiration.

riskCards.risk: A local volume is mistaken for replicated durable storage.
symptoms: One region or host issue takes down stateful service.
Scaling multiple instances fails or causes data divergence.
New Machine cannot automatically see the same files.
riskCards.impact: Downtime or data loss during host, region, or scaling events.
firstChecks: Is the volume local to one host or region?
Can multiple replicas mount it safely?
Does the provider replicate the data automatically?
mitigation: Use managed PostgreSQL for shared state.
Keep off-platform backups.
Do not scale stateful local-volume services without a replication design.

riskCards.risk: Only first launch is tested; redeploy is not tested.
symptoms: The first demo works.
After image update, the instance is empty.
Webhook URL or credentials change unexpectedly.
riskCards.impact: A normal deployment becomes a data-loss event.
firstChecks: Has a redeploy smoke test been run?
Were workflow and credential records checked after redeploy?
Was the same custom domain still shown in webhook nodes?
mitigation: Create a workflow and test credential.
Trigger redeploy.
Verify workflow, credential, execution history, and webhook URL after redeploy.

riskCards.risk: The editor opens, but public webhook URL points to the wrong base URL.
symptoms: Webhook node shows localhost, http, a temporary provider domain, or an old domain.
OAuth callback fails.
External services call a stale endpoint.
riskCards.impact: The instance looks healthy but integrations fail.
firstChecks: Is WEBHOOK_URL set to the stable HTTPS custom domain?
Is N8N_EDITOR_BASE_URL set consistently?
Does DNS/TLS work for the chosen domain?
mitigation: Use a stable custom domain for production.
Set WEBHOOK_URL and N8N_EDITOR_BASE_URL explicitly.
Update OAuth provider callback URLs.

riskCards.risk: Secrets are not backed up or are rotated without a plan.
symptoms: Existing credentials cannot decrypt.
OAuth tokens fail after redeploy.
DB password changes but dependent service is not redeployed.
riskCards.impact: Credential outage and possible permanent access loss.
firstChecks: Where is N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY stored?
Can the team recover DB credentials?
Does provider require redeploy after secret changes?
mitigation: Keep N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY in platform secrets and password manager.
Back up DB before secret rotation.
Document service redeploy order after secret changes.

riskCards.risk: Binary data is written to a non-persistent path.
symptoms: Downloaded attachments disappear.
File processing workflows fail after restart.
Read/Write Files from Disk workflows are inconsistent.
riskCards.impact: Workflow outputs become incomplete or unrecoverable.
firstChecks: Do workflows handle files, images, PDFs, or attachments?
Where does n8n store binary data?
Is that path in a persistent volume?
mitigation: Place binary data path on durable storage.
Use external storage where supported and licensed.
Add binary-data workflows to redeploy smoke tests.

riskCards.risk: Execution history grows without retention control.
symptoms: Database size climbs every week.
Backups become slow or expensive.
Queries and editor pages become sluggish.
riskCards.impact: Cost and reliability degrade over time.
firstChecks: Is execution pruning enabled?
What is monthly execution volume?
What is the database storage trend?
mitigation: Configure execution data pruning.
Monitor database size.
Align retention with compliance and debugging needs.
